mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in >65% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days due to recession fears.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or t-stat for return difference is < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases or interaction term coefficient is insignificant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during expansion regimes or returns are negative in >60% of rising INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with stable unemployment rates create a favorable liquidity and employment environment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect reverses sign or 20-day returns are negative in >50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent signals severe demand contraction, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in >65% of low-GDP-growth quarters or event study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained labor market strength, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over 3-year rolling windows or positive payroll months show negative forward returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 2.5 percent annually forces central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation periods produce positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases or inflation coefficient is positive in regression.
